texto: Didn’t I stand there once, white-knuckled, gripping the just-lit taper, swearing I’d never go back? And hadn’t you kissed the rain from my mouth?And weren’t we gentle and awed and afraid, knowing we’d stepped from the room of desire into the further room of love? And wasn’t it sacred, the sweetness we licked from each other’s hands? And were we not lovely, then, were we not as lovely as thunder, and damp grass, and flame?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Cecilia Woloch que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Didn’t I stand there once, white-knuckled, gripping the just-lit taper, swearing I’d never go bac…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, anniversary, love, com foco em casamento e dia-chuvoso.
